note: SoA buffer layouts — the source rows for the generated BufferLayout keystone. Each buffer is a struct-of-arrays column store for ONE domain type, resident in the Rust worker's linear memory and mirrored read-only by JS. Columns are pure column-major: a column's data is `capacity` contiguous elements at `byteOffset`, so a typed-array view (Int32Array/Uint32Array/...) cuts over exactly one column with zero copy. Edit here; tooling/codegen emits the identical byte-offset table to Rust and TS so the writer and reader provably cannot drift.

buffers.note: The first end-to-end slice: one row per framing member emitted by the FramingSolver. Linear geometry is integer ticks (1/32in); coordinates are wall-local elevation (x along the baseline, z up) — composing the wall→world transform is a later pipeline stage. Each member is a segment (start→end) plus a draw width and an enumerated role.

columns.note: Start point X (along the wall baseline), ticks.
Start point Y (through-wall depth), ticks; 0 in the elevation slice.
Section draw width (stroke thickness in elevation), ticks.
Index into `roles` below — the member's framing role, for coloring and picking.
